items.title: You may have heard of looksmaxxing but what about mummymaxxing?
items.summary: Also known as mommymaxxing and mommaxxing — depending on which internet rabbit hole you're going down — mummymaxxing focuses on optimising motherhood and experts warn it could be harmful.

items.title: Housing Minister denies tax changes are major driver for market 'correction'
items.summary: Clare O'Neil has acknowledged that the property market is undergoing a correction, but says the government's proposed tax changes are not the sole cause.

items.title: When are the Socceroos playing Paraguay? What time is Australia's World Cup game?
items.summary: The Socceroos have one more match in the group stage as they look to make it to the knockout stages of the World Cup. Here's everything you need to know.

items.title: FIFA World Cup: Socceroos should play Mat Ryan against Paraguay, Mark Schwarzer says
items.summary: As the Socceroos prepare to face Paraguay in their final FIFA World Cup group game, Aussie great Mark Schwarzer says now is the time to bring captain Mat Ryan back into the team.

items.title: Coroner warns of dangers of power boards after fatal Carisbrook house fire
items.summary: A Victorian coroner has issued a warning over the safety of power boards following a house fire that claimed the life of a 77-year-old man.

items.title: Matt Wright sued for negligence after airboat he operated allegedly 'collided with a tree'
items.summary: A man who says he suffered a "psychiatric injury" after witnessing injuries sustained by his wife in a 2023 airboat crash is suing tour operator and Netflix star Matt Wright for negligence.

items.title: Two women charged after allegedly trafficking cocaine, meth to Tasmania on Spirit ferry
items.summary: Two women are alleged to have had traffickable quantities of meth and cocaine in their car when they evaded police upon exiting the Spirit of Tasmania ferry in Devonport.

items.title: Baby Kobi's death at Whispering Wall 'preventable' as coroner highlights 'errors'
items.summary: A coroner finds the "upsetting" death of a nine-month-old baby in the Barossa Valley in a public murder-suicide by her "manipulative, self-absorbed and dangerous" father could have been prevented.

items.title: Listen to David Dowsett on ABC Wide Bay to win double passes to Brolga Theatre show
items.summary: Tune in to David Dowsett for ABC Wide Bay's breakfast show and your chance to win a double pass to a show of your choice at the Brolga Theatre.

items.title: Pauline Hanson says her vision of 'monoculture' is like the Socceroos
items.summary: One Nation leader Pauline Hanson has likened her vision of a "monocultural" Australia to the Socceroos as Opposition Leader Angus Taylor clarifies that he would still seek "a version" of multiculturalism.

items.title: Jessie James Tumaliuan sentenced to 26 years' jail for estranged wife's murder
items.summary: Jessie James Tumaliuan has been sentenced to more than two decades in jail for the violent murder of his estranged wife in March last year.

items.title: Ebola virus outbreak in Democratic Republic of Congo one of worst recorded
items.summary: There are now more than 1,000 confirmed cases of Ebola in the Democratic Republic of Congo and 267 people have died, local officials say.

items.title: Papua New Guinea reverses ban on Australian poultry imports due to bird flu
items.summary: Australia's largest chicken meat buyer, Papua New Guinea, has reversed its decision to suspend Australian poultry and egg imports due to the H5N1 bird flu outbreak.

items.title: Daryl Braithwaite's heartbroken by fans' appreciation after announcing end to live performances
items.summary: The ARIA Hall of Fame inductee speaks emotionally to the ABC about his decision to give up live performances, saying it has broken his heart to see how appreciative people have been.

items.title: World Cup quick hits: Cristiano Ronaldo's records, hydration break update, Donald Trump's special job
items.summary: Cristiano Ronaldo makes a bit of World Cup history as Gianni Infantino offers an update on the future of the hydration breaks and Donald Trump is given role at this year's final. Here are today's quick hits.

items.title: Bondi Beach terror attack hero Ahmed Al Ahmed pleads not guilty to alleged assault on father
items.summary: Ahmed Al Ahmed has pleaded not guilty to allegations he assaulted and intimidated his father during an incident in March.

items.title: World Cup: Why Socceroos won't be playing for a draw against Paraguay
items.summary: A draw would be enough for the Socceroos to progress from their World Cup group, but according to defender Jason Geria, playing for anything less than a win does not sit right with this group.

items.title: Limestone Coast farming couple Brian and Val Collins celebrate 70 years of love on the land
items.summary: Farming couple Bill and Val Collins share their secrets to a long marriage as they celebrate their platinum wedding anniversary.

items.title: Karl Stefanovic's interview with Tommy Robinson pulled from social media and podcast platforms
items.summary: Today show host-turned-podcaster Karl Stefanovic has had his interview with far-right extremist Tommy Robinson taken down from social media and podcast platforms.

items.title: Fossil find plugs huge gap in Australia's marsupial record
items.summary: A few teeth found in an outback fossil deposit have helped fill in a 30-million-year blind spot in Australia's evolutionary history.

items.title: Caretaker of Townsville apartment building found guilty of rape, sexual assault
items.summary: Four women who accused Alexander Terry of sexual assault and rape gave evidence in a District Court trial.

items.title: Kangaroos dominate Australia's AFLW squad for Ireland international
items.summary: North Melbourne has the most players of any club selected in Australia's squad for the inaugural AFLW representative match against Ireland.

items.title: World Cup: England's hunt for positivity more fruitful than an attacking spark against resolute Ghana defence
items.summary: England say they need to stay positive after a dour goal-less draw against Ghana, positivity that is hopefully easier to find than shots on target were against the Black Stars' low block in Boston.

items.title: Biosecurity Tasmania on alert over first detection of invasive serpentine leafminer
items.summary: The insect has been found at a commercial grower in Tasmania's north-west. It damages the leaves of the plants it attacks and can reduce crop yield.

items.title: Chemical exposure limits put on hold despite Safe Work Australia advice
items.summary: A majority of state and territory governments have put stricter workplace exposure limits on hold for nine hazardous chemicals.

items.title: Pilbara's North Pole Dome confirmed again to be world's oldest known asteroid crater
items.summary: Researchers find a crater in WA's East Pilbara is 3 billion years old, but it has sparked debate in the scientific community.

items.title: Siri Hustvedt reflects on the death of her husband, Paul Auster, in her new memoir, Ghost Stories
items.summary: The author of What I Loved has published a moving memoir drawing on letters and journals documenting the last days of Paul Auster, her partner in life and writing.

items.title: Andy Burnham describes his first day back in parliament
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Six men charged over alleged insider fraud plot against construction giant Shadforth
items.summary: Two Queensland men are sentenced after pleading guilty to defrauding a major Queensland construction company in an insider plot.

items.title: Latest inflation data increases rate-hike risk, ASX rises — as it happened
items.summary: The chances of an interest rate increase at the next RBA meeting have risen after the release of mixed inflation data by the ABS. The Australian share market finished the day higher despite a major sell-off across US tech. Look back on our live blog.

items.title: Australia records 113-run win over Pakistan to remain unbeaten at Women's T20 World Cup
items.summary: Australia cruises to a comfortable 113-run victory over Pakistan at Headingley, but there may be concern over a finger injury to one of their stars.

items.title: Carley Scott wins long-running Federal Court case against space start-up Equatorial Launch Australia
items.summary: Once a promising space start-up chief executive, Carley Scott was terminated from her job and left millions out of pocket. Now she's finally found vindication, with a court ruling in her favour.

items.title: Funding in NSW budget for 'world-class' robotic surgery in Orange
items.summary: A long-running campaign to secure robotic surgery in western NSW has succeeded with the NSW budget allocating almost $10 million for equipment.

items.title: Australian biosecurity investigates suspected illegal sales of imported peanuts
items.summary: Australian biosecurity authorities are probing claims imported raw peanuts are being sold illegally without quarantine checks and exposing the nation's own peanut industry to potential plant diseases.

items.title: Pauline Hanson says Socceroos represent her vision of monoculture — as it happened
items.summary: Hanson says she is "delighted" monoculturalism is being debated and says the Australian soccer team represents her vision for a national "monoculture". Take a look back at how the day unfolded.

items.title: Grandfather recounts aftermath of fatal Pakistani police shooting of Perth girl Hania Ahmed
items.summary: The grandfather of Australian schoolgirl Hania Ahmed recounts the aftermath of her fatal shooting, as a senior Pakistani lawyer takes aim at the "uncontrolled" police department at the centre of her death.

items.title: Australia's Emerson Jones and Storm Hunter advance in Wimbledon qualifying
items.summary: Emerson Jones and Storm Hunter advance to the second round of Wimbledon's qualifying stage, but some of their fellow Australians are not as fortunate.

items.title: Gilmour Space Technologies' rocket launch ended shortly after lift-off
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Gilmour Space builds defence capability with first hypersonic rocket test in North Queensland
items.summary: Hypersonic rockets will be used to test Defence and science equipment in extreme conditions.

items.title: Half a billion litres of improperly treated sewage released into Tasmanian waterways in a year
items.summary: More than half a billion litres of improperly treated sewage were released into the state's waterways in the 2024 to 2025 financial year, including at popular swimming spots such as Launceston's Cataract Gorge.

items.title: NSW budget offers short-term sugar hit but there could be bitterness to come
items.summary: Faced with rising interest rates, high grocery prices and bloated bills, the cost-of-living help announced in Tuesday's budget will only make a minor dent in household budgets, Nick Dole writes.

items.title: World Cup: Cristiano Ronaldo roars back to life, with his moments off the ball the most telling
items.summary: Cristiano Ronaldo came into Portugal’s second World Cup group game under a firestorm of criticism. He answered with two goals, and important signs he is willing to be a team player.

items.title: US Senate calls for Trump to end Iran war in bipartisan war resolutions vote
items.summary: It is the 10th time the Senate has tried to stop the war, and the outcome, on a vote of 50–48, is a stunning turnaround from past efforts.

items.title: Mount Buller snow school faces an uncertain future after government decision
items.summary: A snow school on Mount Buller faces an uncertain future following a government decision not to open it for enrolments this winter.

items.title: Tasmania Devils VFL captain Jye Menzie on growing up in Bridgewater
items.summary: Jye Menzie grew up in the north of Hobart, known for some of the nation's worst socio-economic outcomes and a high level of disadvantage. But the Tasmania Devils VFL captain is proud of where he comes from, and would not have it any other way.

items.title: Schools close, railways urge against travel as Europe heatwave intensifies
items.summary: Schools are being ordered to close in the UK and railways are urging against non-essential travel across the continent as record-breaking heat takes hold.

items.title: Conservation groups 'concerned' by exotic pet increase call for greater regulation
items.summary: Australian and international authorities are "struggling" to keep up with regulating and policing the exotic pet market as demand surges.

items.title: National Suicide Prevention Outcomes Map to collect data about what services work
items.summary: Policymakers, mental health experts and those working on the front line will use the National Suicide Prevention Outcomes Map to gauge what services are working to prevent people from reaching critical crisis points.

items.title: Tax experts and self-managed super funds criticise Labor–Greens deal as 'policy on the run'
items.summary: A ban on self-managed super funds borrowing money to buy housing has blindsided the sector, which says Labor should instead target "unscrupulous" operators spruiking property as an investment.

items.title: Port Moresby's water supply network showing 'signs of imminent failure'
items.summary: A decades-old pipeline supplying water to nearly 1 million people in Papua New Guinea's capital is at risk of collapsing without urgent repairs, threatening to cut off more than half of the city's water supply.

items.title: Gardening Australia star proud to be out after being 'de-gayed for TV for 26 years'
items.summary: Gardening Australia star Jerry Coleby-Williams hid his identity on TV for 26 years, but has never been ashamed of being a gay man.

items.title: Cosy ways to keep warm this winter without turning the heater on
items.summary: Heating your whole house — or even a room — can be expensive in the depths of winter. Keeping the humans inside it warm is often cheaper and more efficient.

items.title: Travel quiz, June 24: Join us for a mid-week trip around the world
items.summary: Which town would you be in if you were sunbathing at Cable Beach? Where does Maldon sea salt come from?

items.title: Colombia scores late winner against Congo, England frustrated by resolute Ghana
items.summary: The World Cup looks set for a second 0-0 draw before a late strike by Daniel Muñoz sees Colombia to a 1-0 win over DR Congo to sit atop Group K despite Portugal's 5-0 victory over Uzbekistan.

items.title: Daryl Braithwaite steps back from live performances, saying the joy has gone
items.summary: Australian vocalist, former Sherbet frontman and ARIA Hall of Fame inductee Daryl Braithwaite says he is stepping back from singing live.

items.title: Kids Helpline crisis interventions again on the rise, sparking concern about return to COVID-era levels
items.summary: Kids Helpline says a growing number of Australian young people are requiring immediate emergency help, with a concerning trend around crisis interventions.

items.title: Senior Albanese minister clashes with Pocock over top-secret AI copyright claim
items.summary: Federal politician David Pocock has clashed with a senior Albanese government minister over a whistleblower claim that federal cabinet is considering plans to let AI companies train on Australian copyright material.

items.title: Angus Taylor refuses to commit to a multicultural Australia under repeated questioning
items.summary: The opposition leader, pressed multiple times on whether his party still supported multiculturalism or endorsed One Nation leader Pauline Hanson's vision of a "monoculture", refused to commit to either.

items.title: 'Shaman' and partner withheld magic mushrooms use before Adelaide fashion icon's death, inquest told
items.summary: A coronial inquest is investigating the death of Adelaide fashion icon Carol Foord, who used magic mushrooms as an alternative treatment prior to her death in June 2024.

items.title: World Cup: Alessandro Circati was born in Italy. His dad is happy he chose the Socceroos
items.summary: Alessandro Circati's decision to play for the Socceroos raised eyebrows within his family in Italy, but his father says they now realise that, deep down, he's not Italian.

items.title: SA government moves to regulate AI data centres as water, energy concerns mount
items.summary: The South Australian government is pushing for the state to become home to billions of dollars' worth of artificial intelligence data centres, but says it will seek to regulate their water and energy use.

items.title: Tasmanian egg producers brace for deadly strain of bird flu, potential mass euthanasia
items.summary: Tasmanian poultry producers are ramping up biosecurity measures, as they prepare for the arrival of a deadly strain of bird flu. The state government has stressed that it's well prepared, but says euthanasia is "definitely possible" if there's an outbreak in commercial poultry.
